title: Large magnetoresistance and weak-antilocalization in the nodal-line semimetal VP2
authors: Chunxiang Wu, Shuijin Chen, Tingyu Zhou, Le Liu, Xin Peng, Jianjian Jia, Xinyu Yu, Hangdong Wang, Jinhu Yang, Jianhua Du, Minghu Fang
abstract: After growing successfully high quality VP$_2$ single crystals, we studied systematically their longitudinal $ρ_{xx}(T)$ and Hall resistivity $ρ_{yx}(T)$ at various magnetic fields, combining the electronic band and Fermi surface (FS) calculations. Band calculations reveal that VP$_2$ is a type-II nodal-line semimetal, evidenced by the Hall resistivity measurements. It is found that the magnetoresistance (MR) at higher magnetic fields exhibits a linear behavior and does not show any sign of saturation, reaching 170\% at 40 K up to 9 T, which is determined by the intrinsic electronic structure and dominated by the Lorenz force, demonstrated by the resistivity anisotropy measurements and the numerical simulations. We also found that the existence of small amount magnetic impurities (V$^{4+}$, $S=1/2$, 2.24\%) results in Kondo effect emerging in $ρ_{xx}(T)$, the conductivity at lower magnetic fields exhibits a typical weak anti-localization (WAL) behavior. These results illustrate that VP$_2$ is a platform to study the electronic transport properties of a topological material containing magnetic impurities.
summary: This paper explores the transport properties of the nodal-line semimetal VP2. It demonstrates that the material possesses a type-II nodal-line structure and exhibits large, linear magnetoresistance driven by the Lorentz force. Crucially, the study shows that small amounts of magnetic impurities can induce a Kondo effect and coexist with weak anti-localization. This makes VP2 a significant platform for studying the interplay between topology and magnetic scattering.
main_problem: The study investigates the electronic transport properties and topological nature of the nodal-line semimetal VP2, specifically focusing on the impact of V4+ magnetic impurities on the Kondo effect and weak anti-localization.
main_result: VP2 is identified as a type-II nodal-line semimetal exhibiting large, non-saturating linear magnetoresistance and a Kondo effect induced by 2.24% magnetic impurities, while maintaining weak anti-localization.
method: The researchers combined chemical vapor transport crystal growth, magnetotransport measurements (resistivity and Hall effect), and DFT-based numerical simulations using WannierTools.
model_or_system: The system consists of VP2 single crystals, a type-II nodal-line semimetal with a monoclinic structure. The transport is modeled using a semiclassical two-carrier model and a simplified Kondo model to account for magnetic impurities.
key_observables: Longitudinal resistivity (rho_xx), Hall resistivity (rho_yx), magnetoresistance (MR), magnetic susceptibility (chi), and angular-dependent resistivity.
important_parameters: Kondo temperature (TK = 56.1 K), magnetic impurity concentration (2.24% V4+), and carrier concentrations/mobilities for electrons and holes.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the conductivity correction from weak anti-localization primarily affects the hole diffusion coefficient; the Hall coefficient independence from mobility is treated within a multi-carrier framework.
figures_summary: Figures include temperature-dependent resistivity and susceptibility, Hall resistivity nonlinearity, carrier concentration and mobility fitting results, and angular-dependent magnetoresistance simulations.
paper_structure: The paper begins with crystal growth and structural characterization, followed by electronic band structure calculations, then details magnetotransport measurements, analysis of the Kondo effect and weak anti-localization, and concludes with a discussion on the implications for topological materials with impurities.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. (a) Crystal structure of VP2. (b) XRD pattern of a VP2 polycrystal crystal. Inset: an image of typical single crystals. (c) XRD pattern of a VP2 single crystal. Inset: EDS data of a cleaved sample surface. (d) The calculated Fermi surface of VP2 (e) The nodal-line in first Brillouin zone without considering SOC. (f) high symmetry points in the reciprocal primitive cell. (g) and (k) Fatband calculated without considering SOC (g) and taking SOC into account (k). The colors of the line represents the projectors for different atom orbits and the thickness of the line represents the weight.

Figure 3. (a) Temperature dependence of resistivity of VP2 single crystal under different magnetic fields. (b) MR as a function of temperature under different magnetic fields. Inset: Normalized MR as a function of temperature under different magnetic fields. (c) MR as a function of magnetic field at different temperatures. (d) Fitting of MR using Kohler’s law. (e) Hall resistivity ρyx(B) as a function of magnetic field at various temperatures. (f) Longitudinal conductivity σxx(B) as a function of magnetic field at different temperatures. (g) Fitting of longitudinal conductivity change ∆σxx(B) at T = 2 K. (h) Fitting of Hall conductivity σxy(B) at T = 2 K.

Figure 4. (a) Angular dependence of resistivity ρxx at 2 K for different magnetic fields rotated in the a−c plane. (b) MR as a function of magnetic field magnitude for θ = 0◦, 30◦, 60◦, and 90◦at 10 K. Inset: Schematic of magnetic field orientation. (c) Calculated angular dependence of MR at Bτ = 4. (d) Calculated MR for θ = 0◦.
